title: United Jet Hits Pole, Truck During Newark Landing Approach
summary: A United Airlines Holdings Inc. plane grazed a vehicle and light post on an adjacent highway in its final approach to Newark Liberty International Airport on Sunday, the latest in a string of incidents that have put the spotlight on aviation safety in the US.

title: Supreme Court for Now Restores Abortion Pill Access by Mail
summary: The US Supreme Court temporarily restored the ability of patients to get a widely used abortion pill by mail, pausing a ruling that required people to visit a healthcare provider.

title: JPMorgan Hires Morgan Stanley’s Boyle to Lead PE Secondaries
summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. hired Will Boyle from Morgan Stanley to lead its team that advises private equity firms on structuring private deals, as the bank looks to build out its capital advisory business for sponsors.

title: Asian Stocks Set to Edge Down From Record on Iran: Markets Wrap
summary: Asian stocks looked set to retreat from record highs after the US and Iran exchanged fire, jolting a fragile, four-week-old ceasefire and raising concerns that Middle East tensions could escalate again.

title: Regis to Buy Vault Minerals to Form $7.7 Billion Gold Miner
summary: Australian gold miner Regis Resources Ltd. agreed to buy Vault Minerals Ltd. in an all-share transaction that they said values the combined entity at about A$10.7 billion ($7.7 billion).

title: Gold Holds Losses on Signs That US-Iran Ceasefire Is Fraying
summary: Gold held a loss as the US and Iran traded fire in the Persian Gulf on Monday, jeopardizing a four-week-old ceasefire and fueling inflation risks.

title: Oil Holds Gain as Iran, US Exchange Fire in Middle East Flareup
summary: Oil held the bulk of a sharp gain after tensions flared across the Middle East, with the US and Iran exchanging fire amid renewed attacks on energy infrastructure and vessels in and around the Strait of Hormuz.

title: US and Iran Trade Fire in Gulf, Jolting Four-Week-Old Ceasefire
summary: The US and Iran exchanged fire in the Persian Gulf in a flareup of violence on Monday that also drew in the United Arab Emirates, prompting calls for renewed strikes on Iranian targets and casting doubt on the fate of a four-week ceasefire.

title: Dell Grants COO Clarke Performance Options Worth $132 Million
summary: Dell Technologies Inc. granted Chief Operating Officer Jeff Clarke stock options estimated to be worth $132.4 million in 2031 if the company meets annual performance goals.

title: Investor Michael Burry Says Exited Entire GameStop Position
summary: Famed investor Michael Burry sold his entire position in GameStop Corp. after the gaming retailer made an offer to buy e-commerce company eBay Inc. for about $56 billion in cash and stock.

title: Westpac Profit Misses Estimates as CEO Flags Mideast Risks
summary: Westpac Banking Corp.’s first-half profit missed expectations as Chief Executive Officer Anthony Miller flagged worries about the impact of the Middle East war on customers.

title: Musk Agrees to Pay $1.5 Million Over SEC Twitter Stake Case
summary: Elon Musk agreed to pay $1.5 million to settle Securities and Exchange Commission allegations that he cheated Twitter shareholders in 2022 by failing to properly disclose his growing stake in the social media company.

title: State Street CEO: ETFs Have 'Exploded'
summary: State Street Chairman and CEO Ron O'Hanley discusses the company's strategic approach to the exploding ETF market, 'episodic' M&A, and the impacts of geopolitical risks in the markets. He talks with Katie Greifeld and Romaine Bostick at the Milken Institute Global Conference in Beverly Hills, California. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Starmer Tells UK Leaders All of Society Must Fight Antisemitism
summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer is due to host meetings to tackle antisemitism on Tuesday after two men were stabbed in north London last week in the latest attack targeting British Jews.

title: Guggenheim's Walsh Sees Fed Cutting Once More This Year
summary: Guggenheim Partners Investment Management CIO Anne Walsh discusses the outlook for the equity, oil and fixed income markets. Speaking at the Milken Institute Global Conference in Beverly Hills, California, Walsh sees an extended Iran conflict as the big risk to markets and predicts one more Federal Reserve rate cut this year. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Diamondback Lifting Shale Oil Output Immediately on Rally
summary: Diamondback Energy Inc., one of the biggest shale oil producers, is boosting crude output in response to rising prices caused by the Iran war.

title: The Global Crypto Race Is Leaving a Divided Europe Behind
summary: In the contest over the future of money, the US dollar has a huge head start

title: Pimco's Stracke Sees Client Diversification Away From US
summary: Pimco President Christian Stracke discusses the firm's expansion of its international business. Speaking at the Milken Institute Global Conference in Beverly Hills, California, Stracke says international clients are seeking to diversify away from US markets amid geopolitical shifts. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Emerging-Market Stocks Rise to Record High on Tech Boost
summary: Most emerging-market currencies weakened on Monday as Iran fired missiles at the United Arab Emirates for the first time in almost a month, weakening an already fragile ceasefire, while optimism over artificial intelligence buoyed stock markets in Asia.

title: Kalshi's Quintenz: Prediction Markets are Not Casinos
summary: Former CFTC Commissioner and current Kalshi board member Brian Quintenz discusses the evolving landscape of prediction markets and how federally regulated prediction markets and state-regulated sports books can coexist. He talks with Katie Greifeld and Romaine Bostick at the Milken Institute Global Conference in Beverly Hills, California. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Pretium’s Mullen Says Capital for Rental Homes Is ‘Drying Up’
summary: Legislation that would ban large corporate landlords from acquiring more single-family rental homes is depriving the US housing market of much-needed investment, Pretium founder Don Mullen said in an interview with Bloomberg Television.

title: Pimco’s International Clients Seek to Diversify Away From US
summary: Pacific Investment Management Co.’s international clients are seeking to diversify away from US markets amid geopolitical shifts and as a prolonged equity rally increases their exposure to the world’s largest market, according to Christian Stracke, the company’s president.

title: Sheinbaum Unveils Plan to Attract Investment as Economy Sags
summary: President Claudia Sheinbaum’s government unveiled plans meant to increase private investment into Mexico as she seeks to kickstart an economy that contracted to start the year.

title: Gold Falls as Flare-Up of Hormuz Tensions Fuels Inflation Risks
summary: Gold fell as escalating Middle East tensions threaten a ceasefire between the US and Iran, keeping energy prices elevated and fueling inflation risks.

title: FedEx, UPS Shares Sink on ‘Watershed’ Amazon Logistics Move
summary: US transportation stocks plunged Monday after Amazon.com Inc. announced expanded logistics offerings that will turn it into a major competitor for parcel carriers and air freight companies, and also impact truckers and third-party brokers.

title: Yen’s Brief Spike Leaves Traders on Alert for Intervention
summary: A sudden surge in the yen ensured traders remained on edge over the potential for Japanese authorities to step back into the market after last week’s intervention to curb declines.

title: US Stocks Fall From Record High With Mideast Tensions in Focus
summary: The S&P 500 Index retreated from a historic high on Monday as investors monitored developments in the Middle East and geared up for another busy earnings stretch after solid results helped drive the gauge higher the past five weeks.

title: Pretium CEO: Capital for Home Construction is Drying up
summary: Pretium CEO Don Mullen discusses the impacts of the 21st Century Road to Housing Act, home construction capital drying up, and the need to speed up homebuilding. He talks with Katie Greifeld and Romaine Bostick at the Milken Institute Global Conference in Beverly Hills, California. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SEC Investigating Alleged Fraud in Private Credit, Atkins Says
summary: Securities and Exchange Commission Chairman Paul Atkins said the agency is investigating allegations of fraud in private credit firms, though he declined to say specifically which companies are under scrutiny.

title: Stocks Fall, Oil Rises on Hormuz Tension Flare-Up: Markets Wrap
summary: A rally in oil drove both stocks and bonds lower on concerns that an escalation of hostilities over the Strait of Hormuz will keep energy costs elevated, fueling global inflation risks.

title: US 30-Year Yield Hits 5% as Oil Surge Fuels Bets on a Fed Hike
summary: Treasuries fell, sending 30-year yields to the highest since July, as traders boosted wagers that the Federal Reserve will have to reverse course and raise interest rates to curb inflation following a surge in oil prices.

title: Europe Is Facing Stagflationary Shock, EU’s Dombrovskis Says
summary: European Union Economy Commissioner Valdis Dombrovskis warned of the stagflationary effect that the Iran war is creating for the bloc.
